title: Electronic Cash Based on Batch Groth-Sahai
author: Hamdika, Hamri
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Electronic cash is a form of electronic currency that enables cash transactions over communication networks while ensuring privacy. Electronic money necessitates the verification of a user’s identity to ascertain whether a user of electronic money is legitimate. There are various identity verification methods, one of which is the Groth-Sahai proof. In this study, the Groth-Sahai proof is enhanced to include batch processing steps for identity verification. The results of the batch Groth-Sahai method were utilized in the verification process for electronic money
